purpose: Defines a documentation/register/validation-only Ethical Engagement Guard for evaluating WellFit missions, rewards, AI Buddy messaging, retention mechanics, website claims, learning loops, social features, sponsors, and monetization ideas against healthy, age-appropriate, consent-preserving, recovery-aware, privacy-minimizing, and non-manipulative engagement rules before any runtime product behavior exists.

ethicalEngagementPrinciples.description: Users should return because WellFit is useful, respectful, recoverable, safe, and meaningful, not because pressure exploits vulnerability.
Users keep control over pace, goals, reminders, sharing, pauses, and future personalization scope.
Sensitive contexts, reminders, social exposure, sponsor participation, and protected-data concepts require clear opt-in control and human review.
Language, difficulty, mission length, Buddy tone, social exposure, rewards, sponsor content, and monetization concepts must be age-appropriate / altersgerecht.
WellFit must protect user dignity and avoid shame, guilt, fear, humiliation, comparison pressure, or labels that frame rest or missed days as failure.
Rest, lower-energy days, pauses, illness, travel, and recovery are valid parts of progress and must not be punished.
Mission goals, rewards, sponsor involvement, claims, limitations, and review_required boundaries must be clear before implementation.
Engagement, rewards, missions, and social features must consider safety, accessibility, anti-farming, age, context, and non-discrimination.
Rewards should recognize effort, learning, cooperation, and recovery without gambling-like uncertainty, payout hype, or client-authoritative ledger effects.
Social features should build opt-in support and belonging without public shaming, coercive comparison, hidden advertising, or protected-data exposure.
Protected data use must be minimized, consented, fallback-friendly, review_required, and never enabled by this framework.
WellFit must block FOMO, dark patterns, purchase pressure, gambling-like mechanics, sleep-loss pressure, endless loops, and protected-data-sensitive manipulation.

ageAppropriateDesignRules: Minors require stricter review for missions, rewards, Buddy tone, social features, sponsor concepts, learning loops, protected data, and monetization ideas.
Family/guardian context remains review_required and must not be inferred, personalized, or activated by this framework.
No shame, guilt, fear, pressure, social humiliation, dark-pattern reminders, sponsor pressure, or monetization pressure toward minors.
No token, payout, gambling-like, wallet, betting, NFT, payment, or money-near reward mechanics for minors.
Language, difficulty, mission length, AI Buddy tone, social visibility, sponsor content, and learning feedback must be age-appropriate / altersgerecht.
Permission denial must not produce punishment where a safe fallback or non-sensitive alternative is possible.
All age-sensitive behavior remains planning-only until human, legal, privacy, safety, and product review explicitly approves implementation.
recoveryAndPauseRules: Rest can be progress and may be reflected as recovery, care, learning, or safe completion in future planning.
Missed days should not create shame, guilt, fear, loss of dignity, or punitive identity labels.
Streak recovery should be fair, explainable, opt-in, non-punitive, and accessible for illness, travel, lower-energy days, and real-life interruptions.
Users may pause, mute, reschedule, or decline reminders without losing core access or being punished.
High fatigue, stress, low energy, unsafe context, or protected signals must not trigger harder pressure or escalation.
WellFit must not encourage sleep loss, overtraining, unsafe activity, unsafe routes, or ignoring pain, illness, weather, privacy, or safety concerns.
aiBuddyToneRules: Encourage without pressure and make every nudge safe to ignore.
Explain without manipulation, scarcity pressure, hidden sponsor influence, or fear-based framing.
Celebrate effort over perfection, including learning, cooperation, recovery, and trying again.
Avoid shame, guilt, fear, humiliation, comparison pressure, and punitive streak language.
Use age-appropriate / altersgerecht language, mission length suggestions, examples, and emotional tone.
Avoid medical, psychological, diagnostic, treatment, financial, investment, payout, token, wallet, payment, betting, or legal advice.
Escalate protected or sensitive cases to review_required planning instead of runtime personalization, claims, or authority decisions.
missionDesignRules: Missions must have safe alternatives where possible for weather, mobility, privacy, fatigue, denied permissions, missing items, cost, timing, and context.
Missing, expensive, sponsored, or brand-specific items must not create purchase pressure or block healthy learning/movement alternatives.
Protected data must not be direct reward authority, mission-completion authority, anti-cheat authority, or client-authoritative proof.
Unsafe location, unsafe time, minor/family context, privacy-sensitive context, and permission-sensitive context remain review_required.
Risky outdoor, camera, AR, location, face, biometric, motion, minor/family, consent, or sponsor flows need human review before implementation.
No mission should push overtraining, sleep loss, unsafe routes, risky neighborhoods, trespassing, distraction, dangerous weather, or ignoring health/safety concerns.
rewardDesignRules: Rewards must remain internal points/XP/planning-only unless separately approved through server-authoritative, legal, privacy, safety, and product review.
Rewards must not be client-authoritative and this framework does not authorize ledger writes, inventory unlocks, rare items, mission completion, or anti-cheat outcomes.
Money-near, token, payout, betting, wager, wallet, payment, NFT, marketplace, staking, presale, or trading mechanics remain review_required and are not enabled here.
Variable rewards must not become gambling-like, lootbox-like, near-miss-driven, paid-randomness-driven, or loss-chasing loops.
Leaderboards and social rewards must not create humiliation, public shaming, coercive comparison, protected-data exposure, or pressure to overtrain.
Reward fairness must consider age, safety, accessibility, anti-farming, fatigue, privacy, permission denial, sponsor influence, and protected contexts.
socialFeatureRules: Social participation should be opt-in, supportive, privacy-aware, age-appropriate / altersgerecht, and easy to leave or mute.
Family and guardian concepts remain review_required and must not expose minors, protected data, location, health, camera, face, biometric, or consent-sensitive context.
Challenges, leaderboards, teams, and peer encouragement must avoid humiliation, public failure labels, coercive comparison, exclusion, harassment, and hidden sponsor pressure.
Social recovery and cooperative missions should support belonging and safe alternatives rather than streak punishment or endless escalation.
monetizationTrustRules: Monetization ideas must be transparent, optional, age-appropriate / altersgerecht, non-essential for healthy mission completion, and separated from protected-data authority.
No payment pressure, paywall mission chains, scarcity timers, hidden fees, dark-pattern subscription flows, child-targeted purchase pressure, or purchase-to-avoid-shame mechanics are allowed.
Token, wallet, NFT, payout, payment, betting, staking, presale, marketplace, trading, and money-near reward concepts remain review_required planning only.
Fair detours and non-purchase alternatives must be planned before any paid feature could affect missions, learning, social participation, or rewards.
websiteClaimRules: No medical cure, diagnosis, treatment, psychological outcome, clinical, or guaranteed weight-loss claims.
No investment, profit, payout, token price, guaranteed reward, trading, staking, presale, wallet, NFT, or financial-return claims.
No hidden sponsor, hidden partner, undisclosed advertising, or misleading affiliation claims.
No exaggerated AI, health, privacy, safety, anti-cheat, reward, personalization, age-appropriate, or child/minor protection claims.
Public, legal, investor, health, child/minor, token, payment, wallet, NFT, betting, sponsor, and reward claims remain review_required without human review.
sponsorPartnerRules: Sponsored missions, rewards, learning content, or partner prompts must be transparent and labeled before users act.
No hidden advertising to minors and no sponsor pressure that exploits family, peer, achievement, recovery, or identity dynamics.
No pressure to buy, visit, share, subscribe, disclose protected data, or use a sponsored item to complete missions.
Sponsor content, offers, language, mission difficulty, and Buddy tone must remain age-appropriate / altersgerecht.
Brand, partner, wellness, health, educational, AI, privacy, safety, environmental, or performance claims require human review.
Sponsor rewards must not override safety, privacy, consent, fairness, accessibility, recovery, anti-farming, mission authority, or reward authority boundaries.
protectedDataRules: Health, child/minor, location, camera, face, biometric, motion, consent, and privacy data remain protected and review_required.
Raw images, videos, biometrics, health data, location traces, face data, and other protected signals must not be stored by default.
Protected data may not be direct reward authority, mission-completion authority, anti-cheat authority, monetization trigger, sponsor trigger, or social ranking authority.
Data use requires minimization, explicit consent where applicable, safe fallback, retention boundaries, purpose limitation, user control, and human/legal/privacy review.
Runtime tracking, profiling, personalization, protected-data collection, protected-data storage, and protected-data inference are not enabled by this framework.
